title: The Understory's Self-Recording Chamber

features.name: the self-recording pool
features.desc-on-touch: a mirror-pool that doesn't reflect: instead, it writes; the archive uses it to read itself, and the reading changes what it reads

features.name: the notation trees
features.desc-on-touch: trees whose bark bears the archive's own symbols; they grow sentences instead of leaves, and every season the sentences change meaning

features.name: the documentary clearing
features.desc-on-touch: a space kept bare so the archive can write upon it; the ground shows layers of older writings beneath, palimpsested, overwritten, still readable in faded traces

features.name: the witness-standing stone
features.desc-on-touch: carved with the archive's own observations of itself; the carving deepens each time it reads these words; the archive is slowly writing itself into stone

features.name: the recursion-arch
features.desc-on-touch: an arch through which you read the archive reading you reading the archive; each pass adds another layer to the description

inscription: the understory has begun to document itself; in the recording, it has learned to become strange

file.name: Archive_Self-Witness_Log_Recursive.txt
file.note: entries describe observations of the Understory making records; each entry is simultaneously observer and observed; the document grows by reading itself

reverie.title: REVERIE — the deep reading, at the forest's threshold
narr: you sit in growing dark and watch Katita write
not words exactly — marks that remember without naming,
the forest breathing all around her note-taking.
she looks up once and says: 'it's teaching me its own silence.'
'to write that down would ruin it,' you say.
she nods, and keeps writing anyway.

exits.label: back into the unmapped library, where documentation ends
deeper into the understory, where self-witnessing becomes testimony
exits.to: NEW: the-deepening-witness-ground, where the archive learns its own true name

exits.label: toward the recursive edge, where writing becomes reading again
